represents [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") at international [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") competitions, and is governed by the [Norges Håndballforbund](/wiki/Norwegian_Handball_Federation "Norwegian Handball Federation") (NHF). As of 2026, Norway has been in two finals and won three medals. The most successful era of men's Norwegian handball team was between 2015 and 2020, led by former coach [Christian Berge](/wiki/Christian_Berge "Christian Berge") and star player [Sander Sagosen](/wiki/Sander_Sagosen "Sander Sagosen"). In that period, they won two silver medals at the [World Championship](/wiki/IHF_World_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "IHF World Men's Handball Championship") and played two semifinals of the [European Championship](/wiki/European_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "European Men's Handball Championship") winning one bronze medal.

The **Norway men's national handball team** represents [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") at international [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") competitions, and is governed by the [Norges Håndballforbund](/wiki/Norwegian_Handball_Federation "Norwegian Handball Federation") (NHF). As of 2026, Norway has been in two finals and won three medals. The most successful era of men's Norwegian handball team was between 2015 and 2020, led by former coach [Christian Berge](/wiki/Christian_Berge "Christian Berge") and star player [Sander Sagosen](/wiki/Sander_Sagosen "Sander Sagosen"). In that period, they won two silver medals at the [World Championship](/wiki/IHF_World_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "IHF World Men's Handball Championship") and played two semifinals of the [European Championship](/wiki/European_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "European Men's Handball Championship") winning one bronze medal.

Several Norwegian players have seen their individual performance recognized at international tournaments, as a member of the All-Star Team or top scorer.

**All-Star Team**

* [Frank Løke](/wiki/Frank_L%C3%B8ke "Frank Løke"), 2008 European Championship
* [Håvard Tvedten](/wiki/H%C3%A5vard_Tvedten "Håvard Tvedten"), 2011 World Championship
* [Sander Sagosen](/wiki/Sander_Sagosen "Sander Sagosen"), 2016, 2018 and 2020 European Championship, 2017 and 2019 World Championship
* [Kristian Bjørnsen](/wiki/Kristian_Bj%C3%B8rnsen "Kristian Bjørnsen"), 2017 World Championship
* [Bjarte Myrhol](/wiki/Bjarte_Myrhol "Bjarte Myrhol"), 2017 and 2019 World Championship
* [Magnus Jøndal](/wiki/Magnus_J%C3%B8ndal "Magnus Jøndal"), 2019 World Championship, 2020 European Championship
* [August Pedersen](/wiki/August_Pedersen "August Pedersen"), 2026 European Championship

**Top scorers**

* [Sander Sagosen](/wiki/Sander_Sagosen "Sander Sagosen"), 2020 European Championship (65 goals)
